channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Seven key factors that contribute to healthy ageing in Australia
item.description: New research from Federation University, led by psychologist Dr Chloe Waddell, has highlighted that ageing well in Australia is about more than just physical health. Interviewing and surveying 722 Aus...

item.description: The ongoing conflict between Iran and Israel has seen rapid developments in the first days of April 2026. U.S. President Donald Trump stated that the war with Iran may be concluded within two to three...

item.title: How to Observe Visita Iglesia: Choosing Between 7 or 14 Churches
item.description: Visita Iglesia is a Holy Week tradition observed by Catholics, where the faithful visit multiple churches to pray, reflect, and spend time before the Blessed Sacrament. This practice usually occurs on...

item.title: CS Duale Clarifies Status of Health Claims and Fraud Investigations
item.description: The Kenyan government has sought to reassure citizens about the performance of the Social Health Authority (SHA) amid concerns over alleged unpaid health debts. Health Cabinet Secretary Aden Duale sta...

item.title: Adrian Searle Reflects on Three Decades of Art Criticism at The Guardian

item.title: Lagos State extends tax filing deadline to April 14 to help residents file correctly
item.description: The Lagos State Internal Revenue Service (LIRS) don announce say dem extend the deadline for individuals to file their annual income tax returns from March 31 go April 14, 2026. The move na to give re...

item.title: Africa Needs Urgent Action to Stop $88 Billion in Illicit Financial Outflows
item.description: Oluwale Edun, Nigeria's Minister of Finance and Coordinating Minister of the Economy, don yan say Nigeria and other African countries gats take quick action to stop the $88 billion wey dey lost yearly...

item.title: Handling Misdelivered Mail: Your Legal Rights Explained
item.description: Many households experience the frustration of receiving post addressed to former occupants. According to legal expert Dean Dunham KC, residents are not obliged to act as unpaid postal intermediaries f...

item.description: The Orange Art Project, a nonprofit based in Cape Town, is bringing creative opportunities to children living in foster care across the city. Established during the Covid-19 lockdown in 2020, the orga...

item.title: Bosnia qualify for 2026 World Cup as Italy fail again in penalty shootout
item.description: Italy don face another heartbreak as dem go miss di 2026 FIFA World Cup after Bosnia and Herzegovina knock dem out with 4-1 penalty win for Zenica. Di match start well for Italy, as Moise Kean give de...

item.title: Canada and Mexico pursue distinct strategies as USMCA review approaches
item.description: With the USMCA review set for July 1, Canada and Mexico are approaching the process differently. Mexico has already started technical discussions with the U.S., focusing on vehicle content rules, supp...

item.title: Calgary Property Taxes to Rise by 8.1% Due to Provincial and Municipal Changes
item.description: Calgary homeowners will experience a significant increase in property taxes this year, primarily driven by the provincial government's increased share. City council recently finalized an overall resid...

item.title: The Real Life of Tanzanian Digital Creators: Challenges, Adaptation, and Growth

item.title: MK Stalin launches Tamil Nadu election campaign, addresses key political issues
item.description: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister MK Stalin has officially launched his election campaign ahead of the upcoming state assembly polls. During his campaign event, he highlighted the government's achievements in...

item.title: Canada hikes permanent residence fees for Nigerians and other foreign applicants from April 30, 2026
item.description: Canada don announce say e go increase the fees wey people dey pay to apply for permanent residence, and this one go start from April 30, 2026. Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) talk ...

item.title: Growing Concentration of Fertilizer Ships Raises Trade and Security Concerns in Persian Gulf
item.description: A growing number of fertilizer-laden ships has been gathering in the Persian Gulf, signaling potential shifts in regional trade dynamics amid heightened tensions linked to the ongoing Iran conflict. A...
